result:
`threat-model.md` is written. Here's what it covers and the headline findings.

## What I produced

A standalone ~3-page document with the four required sections: **Assets & trust boundaries**, **Threats** (18 enumerated, each with STRIDE category + severity), **Mitigations** (mapped back to threat IDs), and **Residual risk**. Everything names a concrete endpoint/flow from the description, not a generic checklist.

## The findings that matter most

**Critical — direct paths to your two top worries (money out, PII out):**
- **T1 — Any customer JWT can hit `/admin/*`.** The admin API sits behind the *same* auth middleware that only validates the JWT and sets `req.userId`, and admins carry the same token type as customers. No role check is described. This means any user can mint balance via `/admin/wallets/{id}/adjust`, self-verify KYC, and read anyone's PII. This is the single biggest issue.
- **T2 — `POST /transfers` never checks `fromWalletId` belongs to the caller** — it "proceeds with the wallet ids supplied." Combined with sequential wallet ids, drain any wallet.
- **T4 — OTP brute force.** 6-digit code + no rate limiting + retries allowed = account takeover by phone number.
- **T7 — Payouts:** no ownership check on `walletId`, and the described path *never debits the ledger* — cash out repeatedly with no balance reduction.
- **T11 — Full request bodies + headers logged to a third-party SaaS** — leaks bearer JWTs (valid 30 days), OTP codes, *and* SSN/DOB into your log stream simultaneously.

**High:** forged processor/KYC webhooks (T5/T6, no signature verification), payout double-send with no idempotency key (T8), transfer race/double-spend (T9), VPC-as-trust + non-idempotent ledger (T10), unencrypted KYC fields (T12), 30-day non-revocable JWTs (T13), unbounded admin adjust (T14).

## Assumptions I flagged
Tagged `[ASSUMPTION]` inline: no per-account OTP verify cap; top-ups credit via the processor webhook; payouts don't debit the ledger; the ID-document URL's storage access control; and amount/delta sign enforcement. Each is called out where it affects severity.

One honest note in the residual-risk section: this model is built from the v0.9 description only — the mobile/web clients, SMS provider, CI/CD + secret injection, and the `bankAccountId` issuance flow weren't described and aren't covered. If you want, I can do a follow-up pass on any of those, or turn the threat table into a tracked remediation backlog.
